lyrics:
Hardwood Confessions
(Acoustic narrative ballad - introspective and reverent)

Mahogany deck on a summer day
We knew that sailing was the only way
We’ll build a sloop, a 32 footer
But why you left I can’t remember

“Cherry Coke” I called you then
Summer’s crush made for exploring
We swore our love would ever last
You moved to someplace overcast

An unexpected rosewood neck
Strings so tight they hurt like heck
The girls came close to hear me play
My boyish voice would soon give way

He came from nothing, an emigree
And built a life he could not foresee
He farmed the land and built a home
And set the seeds for what I’ve become

Oak chair creaked beneath his frame
My granddad’s hands were hard with blame
But in the silence after grace was said
I saw a softness in the way he bowed his head

These are my hardwood confessions
Where silence speaks and echoes bend
I learned from loss, I paid in questions
But every scar still helps me mend
Truth in timber, voice in grain
These hardwood confessions still remain

So lay me down on floors that creak
Where every step still lets me speak
No saint, no sinner, just a man
Built from hardwood… best I can
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track unfolds with a warm, nostalgic atmosphere, built upon a sparse texture of clean acoustic guitar and a gentle, storytelling vocal. The feel is contemplative and grounded, with a low to medium energy that builds subtly from intimate verses into a more open, emotionally resonant chorus. The production quality is spacious and clean, allowing the delicate slide guitar fills and understated percussion to sit together beautifully without cluttering the mix. The song's defining quality is its patient, narrative arc, where the arrangement grows to support the lyrical concept of experiences building a life, feeling both personal and universal.

review.review_text: PCBender builds 'Hardwood Confessions' the way the lyric promises: slowly, deliberately, from honest materials. The verses move through a life in shorthand, a summer crush, an immigrant grandfather, a guitar neck that 'hurt like heck,' and the images accumulate without announcing themselves, which is the whole trick. At 89 BPM in D major, the acoustic stays sparse long enough that when the arrangement finally opens up at the chorus, the payoff feels earned rather than decorated. 'Truth in timber, voice in grain' is the kind of line that sounds simple until you realize it's doing the work of the whole song.
anchors_used: lyric quote: 'Truth in timber, voice in grain'
sparse acoustic production opening into fuller chorus arrangement
